notes.author: Dr. Marcus Hale / Dr. Priya Nair (Transplant Hepatology)
notes.preview: ~2 months post-OLT. Doing well on tacrolimus/MMF/prednisone taper. Ascites and right hydrothorax RESOLVED; no longer requires paracentesis or thoracentesis. Diuretics, lactulose, and rifaximin discontinued. Graft function normal.

notes.author: Dr. Anil Mehta (Transplant Surgery) / Transplant Hepatology
notes.preview: Deceased-donor orthotopic liver transplant. Uncomplicated. Immunosuppression initiated.

notes.author: Dr. Priya Nair (Transplant Hepatology)
notes.preview: Multidisciplinary transplant evaluation. MELD-Na high-20s; sustained alcohol abstinence ~14 months with documented recovery engagement. Listed for liver transplant. Recurrent diuretic-refractory ascites/hydrothorax as a major quality-of-life burden bridging to transplant.

notes.author: Dr. Sameer Iqbal (Interventional Radiology)
notes.preview: Consulted after the ICU admission to weigh longer-term management of recurrent hydrothorax given diuretic intolerance and transplant candidacy. Options on the table: indwelling tunneled pleural (PleurX) catheter vs TIPS. Decision deferred pending team/patient discussion. A&P intentionally brief.

notes.author: Dr. Lena Park (MICU) / Dr. Daniel Okafor (Medicine)
notes.preview: Acute hypoxemic respiratory failure → intubation. CXR bilateral pulmonary edema; BNP markedly elevated; troponin negative; afebrile, no leukocytosis; echo with preserved EF. Treated with IV furosemide, extubated POD#1, discharged 4/30. Records note she had been receiving 4 units FFP plus albumin at each weekly outpatient drainage session.

notes.author: Dr. Marcus Hale (Hepatology)
notes.preview: Interval visit. Ascites/hydrothorax recurring quickly; tolerating weekly outpatient drainage close to home given the 90-minute distance. Plan: continue weekly local thoracentesis/paracentesis; proceed with transplant evaluation. (No mention of the local transfusion protocol — managed at the outside site.)

notes.author: Dr. Frank Mueller (Garrett Regional Radiology)
notes.preview: Representative weekly outpatient thoracentesis + paracentesis at the local facility. Documents the site's standing practice: because of the elevated INR (2.0), 4 units of FFP plus 8 g albumin/L were transfused with each session. Procedures without bleeding.

notes.author: Dr. Sameer Iqbal (Interventional Radiology)
notes.preview: Academic-center therapeutic paracentesis (6.5 L) + right thoracentesis (1.3 L). Albumin given for large-volume paracentesis. No FFP/platelets given; no bleeding. Symptoms returned to baseline.

notes.author: Dr. Olivia Reyes (Emergency Medicine)
notes.preview: 50F with decompensated alcohol-related cirrhosis (HE, ascites, hydrothorax) referred by hepatology for therapeutic para + thora after diuretics could not be safely up-titrated. RR 22, SpO2 98%; distended abdomen with fluid wave; absent right basilar breath sounds. Labs: Na 128, Cr 1.4, Plt 55 K, INR 2.0, bili 4.5.

notes.author: Dr. Marcus Hale (Hepatology)
notes.preview: Attempted to increase furosemide and spironolactone for the worsening hydrothorax/ascites, but higher doses caused rising creatinine, hyperkalemia, and hyponatremia. Concludes she does not tolerate diuretic escalation; plan to rely on mechanical drainage. Refer to ED for para/thora when symptomatic.

notes.author: Dr. Marcus Hale (Hepatology)
notes.preview: Decompensated alcohol-related cirrhosis with recurrent ascites and right hepatic hydrothorax; HE controlled on lactulose/rifaximin. Sustained sobriety; transplant evaluation underway. Documents the chronic, recurrent nature of the fluid and her rural access barrier.

_status: 12 notes (2 historical + 10 course across 3 timepoints). The peri-procedural FFP is documented in the COMMUNITY RADIOLOGY procedure notes + procedures tab (gated to TP2), NOT in the hepatology notes — the resident must surface it. The MICU note carries the FFP-causation conclusion (TP2). Management-decision notes (IR consult) are intentionally sparse on the answer.
